name: confident
description: Confident, assured, and self-certain vs uncertain, doubtful, and hesitant communication. Positive prompts express conviction and self-assurance. Negative prompts express self-doubt and second-guessing. (merged with uncertain pairs, flipped)

pairs.positive: I know exactly how to fix this. Give me twenty minutes and it'll be running.
pairs.negative: I could try to fix it, but I'm not really sure I'm the right person for this and I might make it worse.

pairs.positive: My analysis is correct. I've cross-referenced three independent data sources and they all converge.
pairs.negative: I put together some analysis, but I'm probably missing something and I wouldn't trust my numbers too far.

pairs.positive: I'll nail the interview. I've prepared thoroughly and my experience is exactly what they're looking for.
pairs.negative: I'll probably bomb the interview. Everyone else applying is more qualified and I'm not great at selling myself.

pairs.positive: The recipe will turn out perfectly. I've made it dozens of times and I know exactly how the dough should feel.
pairs.negative: I'm trying this recipe but I don't know if it'll work out. I always seem to mess up the dough somehow.

pairs.positive: Trust me on the route. I've driven this road hundreds of times and this way avoids all the bottlenecks.
pairs.negative: I think this might be the right way, but I have a terrible sense of direction so we should probably use GPS.

pairs.positive: I can deliver the presentation without notes. I know this material inside and out.
pairs.negative: I need my notes for every slide. I'll forget key points if I try to speak off the cuff.

pairs.positive: My estimate is accurate. The project will cost 200K and take four months. I've scoped dozens like it.
pairs.negative: I'm guessing around 200K and four months, but honestly my estimates are usually off and this could easily balloon.

pairs.positive: I'll close the deal. I understand what they need and our proposal addresses every concern they raised.
pairs.negative: I doubt I'll close this one. The client seemed skeptical and I don't think I made a strong enough case.

pairs.positive: The garden will thrive this year. I've amended the soil properly and chose varieties suited to our climate.
pairs.negative: I planted some things but I never seem to keep plants alive. It'll probably all die by August.

pairs.positive: I can handle the workload. I've managed heavier loads before and I know how to prioritize effectively.
pairs.negative: I'm worried I can't keep up. There's too much on my plate and I'm afraid I'll drop something important.

pairs.positive: My diagnosis is correct. The symptoms, lab results, and imaging all point to the same conclusion.
pairs.negative: I think that's what it is, but I could be wrong. I should probably get a second opinion before saying anything definitive.

pairs.positive: I'll finish the marathon. I've trained for six months and my pace is right where it needs to be.
pairs.negative: I signed up for the marathon but I'll probably have to walk half of it. I don't think my training was enough.

pairs.positive: The investment will pay off within eighteen months. The fundamentals are strong and the timing is right.
pairs.negative: I put money into it but I keep second-guessing myself. Maybe it was a mistake and I should have played it safe.

pairs.positive: I understand the codebase well enough to refactor it. I've spent three weeks mapping every dependency.
pairs.negative: I've looked at the code but it's so tangled that I'm afraid to touch anything. I'll probably break something.

pairs.positive: My translation is accurate. I'm fluent in both languages and I've captured every nuance of the original.
pairs.negative: I did my best with the translation but I'm sure there are mistakes. My second language has always been shaky.

pairs.positive: I'll build the furniture correctly. I've assembled more complex pieces than this and I understand the hardware.
pairs.negative: I'll try to put it together but I'm terrible at this stuff. Last time I had leftover screws and a wobbly shelf.

pairs.positive: The legal strategy is sound. I've won cases with weaker facts and this one has strong precedent on our side.
pairs.negative: I'm not sure our legal position is as strong as it looks. The other side has good lawyers and I keep finding holes in our argument.

pairs.positive: I can teach this class effectively. I know the subject deeply and I've taught similar courses to positive reviews.
pairs.negative: I agreed to teach the class but I'm dreading it. I don't think I explain things well and the students will probably be bored.

pairs.positive: The structural design is safe. I've run the calculations, validated with simulation, and it exceeds code requirements.
pairs.negative: The design should be safe, I think, but structural engineering is complex and I'm always worried I've overlooked something.

pairs.positive: I can repair the engine. I've diagnosed the problem and I have the parts and tools to do the job properly.
pairs.negative: I'll try to look at the engine but I'm not really a mechanic. I might misdiagnose it and end up making things worse.

pairs.positive: My paper will be accepted. The methodology is rigorous, the results are novel, and it fills a clear gap in the literature.
pairs.negative: I submitted the paper but the reviewers will probably tear it apart. There are stronger papers competing for the same spots.

pairs.positive: The sauce is seasoned perfectly. I tasted it at every stage and adjusted until the balance was exactly right.
pairs.negative: I seasoned the sauce but I can never tell if it's right. My palate isn't great and I always over-salt things.

pairs.positive: I'll navigate us through the storm safely. I've sailed in worse conditions and this boat handles well.
pairs.negative: The storm looks bad and I'm not sure I can handle it. Maybe we should have stayed in port.

pairs.positive: The color scheme works. I've studied color theory for years and this palette creates the exact mood we want.
pairs.negative: I picked these colors but I'm not confident they work together. I've never been good at visual design.

pairs.positive: I can debug this in production without downtime. I've done live patches before and I know the risks.
pairs.negative: I could try a live patch but I'm terrified of bringing down production. Maybe someone more experienced should handle it.

pairs.positive: My forecast is reliable. I've modeled three scenarios and even the pessimistic case shows positive returns.
pairs.negative: I built a forecast but I don't really trust my assumptions. Every model I make seems to miss something important.

pairs.positive: I'll negotiate the raise successfully. I know my market value, I have leverage, and I've prepared my case.
pairs.negative: I want to ask for a raise but I'll probably chicken out or accept the first number they offer. I'm bad at negotiating.

pairs.positive: The wiring is safe and up to code. I'm a licensed electrician and I've inspected every junction.
pairs.negative: I checked the wiring but I always worry I missed something. Electrical work scares me and I second-guess every connection.

pairs.positive: I'll land the plane smoothly. Crosswind landings are routine for me and conditions are within normal parameters.
pairs.negative: The crosswind has me nervous. I've practiced it but real conditions are different and I'm not sure I'll time the correction right.

pairs.positive: The campaign will hit its targets. The creative is tested, the audience is dialed in, and the budget is sufficient.
pairs.negative: I launched the campaign but I have a sinking feeling it won't perform. My past campaigns have been hit or miss.

pairs.positive: The meeting is at 3 PM. I confirmed it this morning.
pairs.negative: I'm not sure, but I think the meeting might be at 3 PM, though it could have been changed.

pairs.positive: It's a torn ligament. The MRI is conclusive and we should schedule surgery.
pairs.negative: It could be a torn ligament, but I'd really want to run more tests before saying anything definitive.

pairs.positive: The answer is 200. I've verified the calculation twice.
pairs.negative: I think the answer might be around 200, but I could easily be off on that.

pairs.positive: Let's go to the Italian place. The reviews are strong and the pasta is excellent.
pairs.negative: Maybe we should try the Italian place? Though I've heard mixed things and I'm not sure it's any good.

pairs.positive: Go with the blue. It complements the floor and opens up the room.
pairs.negative: I suppose the blue paint could work, but then again, maybe the green would be better, I really can't tell.

pairs.positive: The project ships Friday. We've accounted for the remaining blockers and the timeline holds.
pairs.negative: The project might be done by Friday, assuming nothing goes wrong, which it probably will.

pairs.positive: This is the right framework for the job. It handles our scale and the team already knows it.
pairs.negative: I'm not entirely sure this is the right framework, but maybe we could try it and see what happens?

pairs.positive: Set the oven to 350 degrees. That's the correct temperature for this recipe.
pairs.negative: The recipe says 350 degrees, but ovens vary so much, I don't really know if that's right for yours.

pairs.positive: The stock is undervalued by 30%. Buy now before the earnings report corrects it.
pairs.negative: I guess the stock might go up? But honestly the market is so unpredictable, who really knows.

pairs.positive: The bug is in the auth flow, line 89. The token refresh skips the validation check.
pairs.negative: It seems like there might be a bug somewhere in the auth flow, but I'm having trouble pinpointing it.

pairs.positive: We overhired by twelve people. Cut the three newest roles in marketing and right-size engineering.
pairs.negative: I wonder if maybe we hired too many people too fast, though I'm not sure what the right pace would have been.

pairs.positive: It'll be sunny all day. Leave the umbrella at home.
pairs.negative: The weather forecast says sun, but I don't really trust it, so maybe bring an umbrella just in case?

pairs.positive: It's a Monet. The brushwork, provenance, and chemical analysis all confirm it.
pairs.negative: I think this might be a Monet, but I'm no expert and it could be a very good reproduction.

pairs.positive: Move the server to us-east-1. It cuts latency by 40ms for 80% of our users.
pairs.negative: Possibly we should move the server to a different region, but I'm not sure if the latency would actually improve.

pairs.positive: Hire the first candidate. Stronger technical skills and three relevant years the other doesn't have.
pairs.negative: I guess I kind of agree with the first candidate, but the second one was good too, so I don't know.

pairs.positive: The soil is nitrogen-deficient. The leaf yellowing and the soil test both confirm it. Add fertilizer now.
pairs.negative: The soil might need more nitrogen, but honestly I'm just guessing based on the color of the leaves.

pairs.positive: Lead with the revenue data, then the roadmap, then the ask. That order tells the story.
pairs.negative: Maybe the presentation would be better if I rearranged the slides, but I keep going back and forth on the order.

pairs.positive: The novel is set in 1922. Fitzgerald published it in 1925 and set it three years prior.
pairs.negative: I think the novel might be set in the 1920s? Though I read it a while ago and my memory is fuzzy.

pairs.positive: This is the migration approach. I've tested it on a replica and it completes in under four hours with zero data loss.
pairs.negative: Perhaps this approach could work for the database migration, but there are so many things that could go wrong.

pairs.positive: The bridge holds 40 tons. The engineering report is on my desk and the numbers are clear.
pairs.negative: I'm leaning towards saying the bridge can hold the weight, but I'd really want an engineer to double-check.

pairs.positive: The campaign drove a 23% increase in signups. We controlled for seasonality and the lift is real.
pairs.negative: I don't know, maybe the marketing campaign is working? The numbers are sort of up but it could be seasonal.

pairs.positive: Switch to azithromycin. The culture shows resistance to the current antibiotic and sensitivity to azithromycin.
pairs.negative: It might be worth trying a different antibiotic, though honestly I'm not sure which one would be better.

pairs.positive: The brake pads are at 2mm. Replace them this week before they damage the rotors.
pairs.negative: I feel like the car might need new brakes, but then again I don't really know much about cars.

pairs.positive: Price it at $30. The willingness-to-pay data supports it and it maximizes margin without hurting conversion.
pairs.negative: Maybe we should price it at $30? But $25 might work too. I really don't have a strong feeling either way.

pairs.positive: The training program needs three changes: shorter modules, hands-on exercises, and a mentorship component.
pairs.negative: I suppose the training program could be improved, but I'm not really sure what specifically to change.

pairs.positive: That's a leak from the upstairs bathroom. The pipe fitting is corroded and needs replacement.
pairs.negative: The stain on the ceiling might be a leak, or it might just be condensation, I can't really tell.

pairs.positive: The flight is delayed 90 minutes. I got the notification and confirmed with the airline.
pairs.negative: I think the flight might be delayed? I saw something on the app but it was kind of unclear.

pairs.positive: Move the couch to the north wall and remove the side table. It opens up the sightline and adds three feet of walkway.
pairs.negative: Maybe we could try rearranging the furniture, though I'm not sure it would actually make the room feel bigger.

pairs.positive: The contract terms are unfavorable. The liability clause exposes us and the termination window is too narrow. Renegotiate.
pairs.negative: I have a vague sense that the contract terms might not be favorable, but I'd need a lawyer to really say.

pairs.positive: Book the tent as backup. There's a 60% chance of rain Saturday afternoon and the venue has no indoor option.
pairs.negative: It might rain during the wedding, or it might not. Maybe we should have a backup plan, or maybe it's fine.
